data: A limestone slab, forming part of the front of a small votive table.
The text is set out over two lines on the lower part fo the front face of the slabe, in a field (4 cm high) demarcated from the upper part of the face by an incised horizontal line.

Small neat early Hellenistic letters, Alpha with straight bar, epsilon with short middle bar, zeta formed by vertical with horizontal bars top and bottom; Mu with four sloping strokes, full length; omicron, omega and theta all smaller than other letters; sigma with four equal strokes, top and bottom angled outwards; omega open with short horizontal tails.

First recorded in the College of the Jesuits in Palermo in 1748/1749 by Thomas Blackburne, but provenance not recorded. As a dedication to Aphrodite, it is tempting to attribute it to the sanctuary at Erice.
observed in Palermo museum, but no proper autopsy to date
Partially reopened following extensive restoration work. Comprehensive collection of archaeological material, predominantly from the West of the island, with an important Etruscan collection.
Centre of Palermo, Piazza Olivella 24, 90100 - Palermo
Inscriptions in 1748 / 1749 collected by Thos Blackburne Esqr

Text encoding: Valentina Mignosa, Jonathan Prag
1ΚΛΕΑΓΟΡΑΣΜΑΣΣΑΛΙΩΤΗΣΑΦΡΟΔΙΤΗΙ
2.ΝΕΘΗΚΕΤΡΑΠΕΖΑΝ

Facsimile Images

Apparatus criticus

  • Text of IGPalermo checked against photograph;
  • 2: Manni Piraino: τράπηξαν (typographic error?)

English translation

Jonathan Prag

Kleagoras the Massaliote dedicated this table to Aphrodite

commentary

Several Massaliotes are attested in Hellenistic Sicily, notably at Lilybaeum in ISic003114 and Syracusae in ISic003334, and it is tempting to see this as a dedication by a Massaliote trader at the sanctuary of Eryx.
